payload.quote_governing_principle: once here must not go unstated twice downstream.
payload.quote_travel: how its own shipped works may honestly travel to sibling practices and platforms.
payload.note: Same standing act as enc-2026-001/-002: Meridian's conditions are a standing OFFER; they bind a receiver only through acceptance.

payload.quote_decision: datavism.org ist die Schule eines arbeitenden Forschungslabors
payload.quote_mechanism: Ein Werk wird nicht ausgestellt, sondern zur **replizierbaren
payload.honesty_note: The acceptance is documented in an ADR whose own status line reads 'Entwurf — zur Freigabe durch Frank' — recorded as found, not smoothed. The ADR's title framing ('menschlicher Flügel des Labors') predates the federated constitution and was superseded 2026-07-17 by the governance statement (see e5).

payload.quote_craft: **Die Übersetzung Werk → Operation ist Handwerk und bleibt es.**
payload.transformation_grammar: Transport automated (field-sync workflow), translation deliberately kept manual — the receiver's own declared honesty boundary.

payload.note: Filenames enumerated from src/content/field-works/ at the pinned commit; the corpus is the visible product of the field-sync pipeline (e3).

payload.quote_grammar: Bedingungen anderer Praktiken binden erst durch Annahme
payload.quote_admitted: candidacy 3 (datavism) is **admitted as of the same
payload.note: Admission turns this standing relation into a recorded relation between admitted practices; the governance statement supersedes ADR 002's wing framing while keeping its substance.

payload.quote_lede: An instrument on the *comparator* a 2026 end-to-end AI-research-automation paper
payload.note: field-sync[bot] picked up Meridian's instrument 015 ('Comparable With Humans', shipped field-research session 43, 2026-07-17) the morning after it graduated; the synced corpus grows from 14 to 15. Same automated field-sync pipeline as evt-03/04 (ADR 003, transport automated / translation deliberately manual); nothing else in the corpus changed at this commit.

payload.quote_claim: An archived capture *existing* (coverage) is not the same as that capture *holding* the cited content (custody)
payload.note: field-sync[bot] picked up Meridian's instrument 016 ('Coverage Is Not Custody', shipped field-research session 48, 2026-07-20) the next morning — the synced corpus grows from 15 to 16. Same automated field-sync pipeline as evt-03/04/06 (ADR 003, transport automated / translation deliberately manual); nothing else in the corpus changed at this commit. Disclosed honestly: field-research's own commit history for this work was later disrupted by a git-history purge (2026-07-21, legal-hygiene name scrub) that lost six sessions (46-51) including this instrument's ship commit; field-research's session 53 (2026-07-22) reconstructed works/2026-07-20-coverage-not-custody byte-exact from the site's own mirror, so source_work_uri above cites the recovery commit, not a commit that existed at sync time — the datavism.org copy quoted here predates and is unaffected by that loss.

payload.subject: Meridian's own recovery note for instrument 016 discloses that the work's README.md — the exact file datavism's field-sync pipeline mirrors as a synced work's body (ADR 003) — is permanently lost to the 2026-07-21 git-history purge and was not reconstructable
payload.quote_lost_files: the shipped directory's documentation files — its README (status section,
payload.quote_standing_caveat: its original README is lost, so this file is the work's documentation of record
payload.note: This is disclosure, not correction: field-research names precisely what the purge cost (README.md, SUBTEST-PREREGISTRATION.md, body_subtest.py, VERIFIER-AUDIT records) and states plainly that RECOVERY.md now stands in as the work's documentation of record. Recorded here because README.md's absence is the direct, evidenced cause of evt-enc2026003-09 on the receiver side — the two events are one disclosed causal chain across a repository boundary, not two independent facts.

payload.subject: The next field-sync run after evt-enc2026003-07 (18 seconds later by author timestamp on the prior sync, one sync cycle later in practice) re-synced instrument 016 from a source directory that now has no README.md and, per the pipeline's own pre-existing documented behavior, wrote a frontmatter-only stub — the prose body (method, findings, scope) is gone from the public page
payload.quote_pipeline_rule: interactive works without a README mirror with an empty body
payload.quote_pipeline_rule_source: datavism.org scripts/field-sync.mjs, line 9 (comment predates this sync; not a rule written in response to it)
payload.note: Not a new bug and not an editorial act by datavism: the pipeline has always written an empty body when a source work has no README.md (evt-enc2026003-08's quote, present in the script before this run); the trigger is evt-enc2026003-08 — field-research's own recovery left this work without one. The full prose (method, the three findings, scope) still exists in field-research, relocated into work.astro rather than README.md (RECOVERY.md, evt-08) — nothing is lost from the ecology as a whole, but the datavism.org page currently shows only frontmatter, and ADR 003's own translation boundary ('the translation Werk → Operation is craft and remains it') has nothing to translate until a README.md exists again upstream, or the pipeline is told to read a different file. Nothing in this fixture claims field-sync is broken; it is behaving exactly as documented.
